label: Cinematic
description: Large-scale, purely instrumental orchestral music designed to evoke powerful emotion and visual storytelling without the need for vocal narratives.
directives: Use a full orchestral palette including strings, brass, woodwinds, and epic percussion. Strictly avoid choirs or vocals of any kind. Build tension and release through dynamic swells — start quiet and let the piece breathe before unleashing its full power. Let sweeping string sections or bold brass horns carry the primary thematic motif. Use layered reverb and a wide stereo mix to fill the space. Tempo should range between 55–90 BPM for emotional depth, or 130–160 BPM for action. Ground the emotional arc in a clear narrative journey: establish, build, climax, resolve.

label: Pop
description: Catchy, polished, and hook-driven instrumental music designed for broad appeal, substituting vocal leads with bright, memorable instrumental melodies.
directives: Lead with a strong, singable melodic hook played by a lead synthesizer, bright electric guitar, or brass section within the first 30 seconds. Absolutely no vocals or voice chops. Structure the piece with clearly defined pop sections — verse, pre-chorus, chorus, bridge. Keep the production modern and polished: use layered synths, programmed drums, and punchy basslines. Tempo should be energetic, typically 100–130 BPM. The mix should be bright, loud, and radio-ready. Keep the mood optimistic and feel-good, letting the lead instrument carry the narrative weight usually reserved for a singer.

label: Indian Contemporary
description: A modern, beat-driven instrumental fusion blending traditional Indian melodic instruments with contemporary global production.
directives: Combine traditional Indian instruments like the sitar, sarangi, or bansuri (flute) with modern Western production elements like deep synth bass, acoustic guitars, and cinematic string layers. Use a rhythm section that blends a standard drum kit or programmed beats with Indian percussion like the dholak or tabla. The tempo should be vibrant and rhythmic, around 90–115 BPM. Ensure the track remains entirely instrumental; rely on the traditional Indian instruments to deliver the primary 'vocal-like' hooks and melodies. Keep the overall mood celebratory, cinematic, or romantically uplifting.

label: Indian Classical
description: Deeply meditative and structured instrumental music rooted purely in the raga system, featuring acoustic textures and complex rhythmic cycles.
directives: Ground the piece in a specific raga scale, completely omitting vocalists (no khayal or dhrupad singing). Use the sitar, sarod, or bansuri as the absolute lead melodic instrument. Support the melody with a tabla or pakhawaj playing a defined tala cycle (e.g., teentaal of 16 beats). Maintain a continuous tanpura drone as the tonal foundation. Structure the piece starting with a slow, free-form exploration (alap) leading into a faster rhythmic section (jod/jhala). Incorporate heavy use of meend (microtonal glides) and gamak. Keep the production highly acoustic, raw, and intimate, capturing the natural resonance of the instruments.

label: Jazz
description: Instrumental jazz emphasizing swing rhythm, complex harmony, and expressive melodic improvisation across acoustic ensembles.
directives: Build the track around an entirely instrumental jazz ensemble — a piano trio, quartet, or quintet. Use a saxophone, trumpet, or electric jazz guitar as the primary lead instrument instead of a vocalist. Rhythmic foundation must feature a human-feeling swing or bossa nova groove. Include jazz chord extensions (7ths, 9ths, 11ths). Anchor the harmony with an upright walking bass line and sparse piano comping. Leave significant space in the arrangement for improvised instrumental solos. Drums should swing lightly using brushes or sticks. Capture a warm, intimate acoustic club feel with slight room ambiance.

label: Afrobeats
description: High-energy, groove-driven instrumental tracks blending West African polyrhythms with modern pop and R&B production techniques.
directives: Create a strictly instrumental track built on a syncopated, polyrhythmic drum pattern fusing traditional percussion (djembe, shakers, woodblocks) with modern 808 kicks and crisp hi-hats. Set the tempo to an uplifting 100–115 BPM. Because there are no vocals, use muted electric guitar plucks, a bouncy bassline, and warm marimba or synth brass sounds to carry the main melodic phrasing. Incorporate call-and-response patterns between different lead instruments. Keep the mix warm, punchy, and dance-floor ready, ensuring the instrumental hooks are catchy enough to drive the song entirely on their own.

label: Lo-Fi / Chill Beats
description: Relaxed, beat-driven instrumental music with intentional analog imperfections, designed as background music for studying or unwinding.
directives: Keep the track 100% instrumental with absolutely no vocal lines or intrusive voice samples. Use a slow, slightly swung boom-bap drum pattern at 70–90 BPM. Layer a warm, detuned Rhodes electric piano, jazzy acoustic guitar, or muted trumpet as the primary melodic voice. Apply intentional imperfections: vinyl crackle, tape saturation, and soft background ambiance (rain, distant room tone). Use heavy low-pass filtering on drums and pads to create a muffled, warm sonic character. Keep the arrangement sparse and repetitive enough to loop seamlessly without causing listener fatigue.

label: Electronic
description: Synthesizer-driven, heavily rhythmic instrumental electronic music relying on electronic sound design, driving bass, and synthetic drums.
directives: Focus purely on electronic sound design without any vocal tracks or vocal chops. Use layered synthesizers (arpeggios, thick basslines, and bright lead plucks) over a steady drum machine pattern (e.g., four-on-the-floor kick drums or syncopated breakbeats). Tempo should be active, ranging from 115–130 BPM. Build energy and dynamic arcs through filter sweeps, LFO automation, and layering of synth textures rather than relying on lyrics or vocal builds. The mix should be tight, wide, and bass-heavy, leaning towards house, synthwave, or techno sensibilities.

label: Ambient
description: Atmosphere-first, beatless instrumental music built on synthesizers, pads, and evolving textures designed for deep immersion.
directives: Create a purely instrumental, beatless soundscape. Prioritize texture, space, and atmosphere over traditional melody or rhythm. Omit drums and percussions completely. Use slow-evolving synthesizer pads, granular drone textures, and gentle pitch drift to create organic movement. Wash the instruments in long, lush reverb and delay to make the piece feel like it exists in a vast, open space. The emotional register should be contemplative, ethereal, or otherworldly. Let the harmonic shifts happen incredibly slowly, allowing the listener to get lost in the instrumental textures.
